attacking_style: Not a primary attacker. Rare solo efforts show unexpected climbing durability and tactical early acceleration creating surprise elements that pure sprinters cannot counter.
team_tactics: Elite leadout pilot delivering sprinters at 100-200m with late release preventing premature launches. Maintains tight centerline geometry through technical corners to block positioning. Sustains high power (60kph+) from 800m when needed.
terrain_preference: Excels on technical, chaotic flat finishes with tight corners requiring precision piloting. Gradient exposure above 2-3% reveals limitations in final 100m leadout sustainability.
vulnerability: Physical pressure from rival leadout men can disrupt lane holding (PEDERSEN example). Struggles to convert individually despite finding correct wheels. Gradient-exposed finishes compromise final 100m effectiveness.
key_patterns: Delivers winning leadouts through chaotic technical finishes at 100-200m (7+ observations)
Late release at 200m prevents premature sprinter launches, demonstrates deliberate timing control (3 observations)
Maintains tight centerline geometry through corners blocking rival positioning attempts (2+ observations)
Sustains 60kph+ power from 800m differentiating elite execution from competent work (2 observations)
Individual conversion struggles despite correct positioning when not leading out others (2 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: 2021-2022 observations show consistent elite-level piloting through chaos, maintaining unmatched technical precision despite train disruptions
